title: Accounts Receivable Turnover
event: Reyes Co. reports net credit sales of $480,000. Accounts receivable were $38,000 at the beginning of the year and $42,000 at year-end. Compute accounts receivable turnover and days' sales uncollected.

computationPaths.narration: Turnover asks how many times the receivables pool converted to cash this year — higher is faster collection.

memorize.body: AR turnover = net credit sales ÷ AVERAGE accounts receivable. Days = 365 ÷ turnover.
Compare days-to-collect against the credit terms: 30 days collected on net-30 terms is healthy; 45 is a problem.

questions.prompt: Net credit sales $480,000, average AR $40,000. AR turnover = ?

distractors.feedback: You divided by ENDING AR (42,000). Flow ÷ snapshot ratios use the AVERAGE balance: 12.
That's DAYS' sales uncollected (365 ÷ 12) — the turnover itself is 12 times.
